varieties.name: Jitlee
varieties.traits: Medium-sized fruit (36-45g), round shape, orange-red to red skin with moderate spintern density
varieties.flesh_fruit: White, thick aril (42% recovery), firm texture, less juicy than other varieties, sweet with subtle acidity (20-21°Brix)
varieties.growing_notes: One of the main varieties grown in tropical Queensland and Northern Territory. Performs well in humid subtropical conditions with protection from cold. Hardy variety suitable for home gardens. Creamy texture distinguishes it from juicier varieties.
varieties.availability: Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery, tropical Queensland nurseries

varieties.name: R134
varieties.traits: Medium-sized fruit (42-45g), ovate shape, bright orange-red skin with good color presentation
varieties.flesh_fruit: White translucent, thick aril, sweet with hint of rose, firm and juicy (14-15% aril, 6.6-7°Brix)
varieties.growing_notes: One of the main commercial varieties in tropical Australia. Recommended for Queensland cultivation. Early flowering, adapts well to tropical Queensland conditions. Excellent market variety due to attractive appearance.
varieties.availability: Queensland commercial nurseries, tropical fruit tree specialists

varieties.name: R156 (Muar Gading)
varieties.origin: Muar, Johor, Malaysia - registered early 1960s by Ng Ah Kow
varieties.traits: Medium fruit (37-60g), round, distinctive ivory/bright yellow skin when ripe, soft pale yellow spinterns, grows 5-6m height
varieties.flesh_fruit: White translucent, very thick aril (57% recovery), firm, juicy, neutral semi-sweet flavor with mild acidity (22°Brix)
varieties.growing_notes: Yellow variety popular in Malaysia. One of main varieties in tropical Australia. Moderately vigorous, early ripening. Unique yellow color makes it distinctive in markets. Thinner skin than red varieties. Performs well in tropical Queensland with proper care.
varieties.availability: Limited in Australia, some tropical Queensland nurseries

varieties.name: R167 (Chai Tow Cheng)
varieties.traits: Large fruit (44-48g), elongated ovate shape, red peel with prominent spinterns
varieties.growing_notes: One of the four main varieties grown commercially in tropical Australia (Queensland and Northern Territory). Commonly used as rootstock due to vigor. Adapts well to tropical Queensland climate with adequate humidity and warmth.
varieties.availability: Queensland commercial nurseries, Northern Territory growers

varieties.name: Binjai
varieties.origin: Binjai region, North Sumatra, Indonesia - traditional variety
varieties.traits: Large fruit (45-54g), elongated shape, striking bright red skin, long spinterns (though somewhat sparse), vigorous tree
varieties.flesh_fruit: White, very thick, soft almost creamy texture, exceptionally sweet with subtle acidity, less watery, freestone tendency

varieties.name: Rongrien (Rong Rien, R191)
varieties.origin: Thailand, selected near a school in Penang, Malaysia ('Anak Sekolah' means 'school child')
varieties.traits: Medium-large fruit (41-45g), perfectly round shape, bright red skin, long flexible spinterns, vigorous growth
varieties.flesh_fruit: White, firm and crisp, exceptionally sweet (16-17%, 7-21°Brix), juicy, satisfying bite texture
varieties.yield_notes: Very high productivity, reliable heavy cropper
varieties.growing_notes: Most popular commercial variety in Thailand. Grown in tropical Australia with success. Flowers later than other varieties in Northern Territory but early in North Queensland. Excellent for fresh eating and holds up well in fruit salads. Requires pollinator as produces few male flowers.
varieties.availability: Limited in humid subtropical climates area, available in North Queensland tropical nurseries

varieties.name: R162 (Daun Hijau / Ong Heok)
varieties.traits: Large fruit (36-59g), elongated shape, yellow-red-orange peel, vigorous tree to 7m
varieties.flesh_fruit: White, very thick (54% recovery), firm, dry, sweet (22°Brix), freestone type
varieties.growing_notes: One of Malaysia's commercial varieties. Grown experimentally in tropical Australia. Round canopy shape. Sensitive to waterlogging. Suitable for tropical Queensland with well-drained soils.

varieties.name: Gula Batu (R3)
varieties.traits: Large fruit (54g), ovate shape, red skin
varieties.flesh_fruit: White, thick (16-17%), sweet like rock sugar (hence name 'sugar rock'), 7.2°Brix
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity, performs well as rootstock

varieties.name: R9
varieties.traits: Medium fruit (37g), oval shape, red-orange skin
varieties.growing_notes: Malaysian variety introduced to Australia. Acceptable in Australian markets due to relatively free testa adherence. Suitable for tropical Queensland cultivation.

varieties.name: R168
varieties.traits: Large fruit (49g), oblong shape, red skin
varieties.growing_notes: Malaysian variety found acceptable in Australian markets. Relatively free from testa adherence issue. Suitable for tropical Queensland.

varieties.name: Arka Coorg Arun 🏆
varieties.origin: ICAR-IIHR Central Horticultural Experiment Station, Chettalli, Karnataka, India - released Indian variety
varieties.traits: Medium fruit (40-45g), early maturing variety, semi-spreading tree, dark red skin
varieties.flesh_fruit: White, thick aril (42% recovery), firm, dry, sweet (20°Brix), freestone - aril cleanly separates from seed

varieties.name: Arka Coorg Pitab (Yellow) 🏆
varieties.origin: ICAR-IIHR Chettalli, Karnataka, India - released Indian variety
varieties.traits: Medium fruit (25-30g), yellow skin when ripe, semi-spreading growth, late bearer, 6-year tree yields 500-1000 fruits
varieties.flesh_fruit: White, thick aril (44% recovery), sweet and juicy, freestone (19.5°Brix)

varieties.name: N18 🧬
varieties.origin: Homegrown Biotech, Kottayam, tropical monsoon climates - Indian commercial hybrid development
varieties.traits: Medium-large fruit (30-40g), red skin, highly adaptable to Indian conditions
varieties.flesh_fruit: White, very thick aril (44% recovery), rich crunchy luscious pulp, soft texture, freestone, sweet (20-21°Brix)
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity, fruits 2-3 years from grafting, average 60-70kg per mature tree (2000-3000 fruits)

varieties.name: HG Rongrien 🧬
varieties.origin: Thailand variety adapted by Homegrown Biotech for Indian conditions
varieties.traits: Medium-large fruit, round, bright red skin, commercial type

varieties.name: HG Malwana 🏆
varieties.origin: Malwana, Sri Lanka - traditional variety adapted for Indian cultivation
varieties.traits: Medium fruit (20-30g), globose shape, red rind and hair, commercial type
varieties.flesh_fruit: White, sweet thick flesh that easily separates from smaller seed, freestone (21°Brix)

varieties.name: HG School Boy
varieties.origin: Thailand/Malaysia variety adapted by Homegrown Biotech
varieties.traits: Medium fruit, red skin, commercial type

varieties.name: HG Baling
varieties.origin: Malaysia variety (Deli Baling/R193) adapted by Homegrown Biotech
varieties.traits: Medium-large fruit, red skin, commercial export quality

varieties.name: HG Jarum Emas
varieties.origin: Indonesia/Malaysia variety adapted by Homegrown Biotech
varieties.traits: Medium fruit, distinctive appearance, commercial type

varieties.name: King 🌱
varieties.traits: Medium fruit, homestead variety

varieties.name: Caesar 🌱
varieties.traits: Medium fruit, red skin

varieties.name: Sri Lankan Rambutan 🏆
varieties.traits: Variable fruit size, typically medium, red or yellow when ripe

varieties.name: Maharlika
varieties.traits: Medium fruit (30-40g), dark red/deep wine-red skin with vibrant red 'hair', perfect-sized
varieties.flesh_fruit: White, thick aril, super sweet and juicy, high sugar content, cleanly separates from seed (freestone), premium taste (21°Brix)
varieties.yield_notes: Good production, fruits 2-3 years from grafting

varieties.name: Lebak Bulus
varieties.origin: Indonesia, West Java - traditional leading commercial cultivar
varieties.traits: Medium fruit (45-53g), round shape, vibrant bright red skin, short soft hairs, shiny appearance
varieties.flesh_fruit: White, thick, exceptionally sweet and mild, very juicy, soft texture, delicate flavor with balance of sweetness and mild acidity (11-12%, 7.3°Brix)
varieties.yield_notes: Good to high productivity, tall vigorous tree

varieties.name: Rapiah (Aceh Rapiah)
varieties.origin: Pasar Minggu, Jakarta, Indonesia - traditional breeding, also called Aceh or Aceh Pelat
varieties.traits: Medium fruit (42g), unique uneven green-yellow-red skin coloration, sparse short harsh hairs, difficult to propagate by marcotting
varieties.flesh_fruit: White, very thick, sweet, somewhat dry texture, firm, chewy (ngelotok), easily removed from seed, freestone (12%, 7.1°Brix)
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate - not very heavy bearer but excellent fruit quality, 1000-1600 fruits per tree (18-30kg)

varieties.name: Sinyonya
varieties.traits: Medium fruit, red skin
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate, requires pollinator as produces 100% female functional flowers

varieties.name: Cimacan (Simacan)
varieties.traits: Medium fruit, red-yellow skin perimeter
varieties.flesh_fruit: White, thick, sweet with soft texture

varieties.name: Aceh Kuning (Yellow Aceh)
varieties.traits: Medium fruit, distinctive yellow skin when ripe
varieties.flesh_fruit: White, thick, honey-like sweetness, less acidic than red types

varieties.name: Silengkeng
varieties.traits: Medium fruit, red skin
varieties.growing_notes: Popular Indonesian cultivar. Well-adapted to tropical conditions with heavy rainfall. Suitable for tropical monsoon climates cultivation.

varieties.name: Sikonto
varieties.traits: Medium fruit, red skin
varieties.growing_notes: Popular Indonesian cultivar suitable for tropical monsoon conditions. Appropriate for tropical monsoon climates cultivation including tropical monsoon climates.

varieties.name: Si Chomphu (Seechompoo)
varieties.traits: Medium fruit, unique pinkish-red color, round shape

varieties.name: Chanthaburi 1
varieties.origin: Chanthaburi Province, Thailand - major production center
varieties.traits: Medium-large fruit, red skin
varieties.growing_notes: Selection from Thailand's major rambutan production center. Well-adapted to tropical monsoon conditions. Suitable for tropical monsoon climates cultivation.

varieties.name: Seematjan
varieties.traits: Medium-large fruit, red skin
varieties.yield_notes: Good production, all hermaphrodite flowers function as female

varieties.name: Seenjonja
varieties.traits: Medium fruit
